# servicenow_jdbc_connection (Resource)

`servicenow_jdbc_connection` manages a JDBC connection configuration within ServiceNow. JDBC connections extend the generic connection table and are used to connect ServiceNow to remote relational databases, optionally through a MID server.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manages a JDBC connection tied to a connection alias and (optionally) a MID server.
resource "servicenow_alias" "example" {
  name            = "example-jdbc-connection-alias"
  type            = "connection"
  connection_type = "jdbc_connection"
}

resource "servicenow_jdbc_connection" "example" {
  name             = "example-jdbc-connection"
  connection_alias = servicenow_alias.example.id
  connection_url   = "jdbc:mysql://db.example.com:3306/app"
  database_name    = "app"
  database_type    = "mysql"
  active           = true
  use_mid_server   = false
  mid_selection    = "auto_select"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `connection_alias` (String) Sys ID of the associated connection alias configuration (type=jdbc_connection).
- `connection_url` (String) JDBC URL for the connection, e.g. jdbc:mysql://host:3306/dbname.
- `name` (String) Display name of the JDBC connection configuration.

### Optional

- `active` (Boolean) If set to 'true', this property will enable the JDBC connection.
- `credential` (String) Sys ID of the associated credential record (typically a JDBC credential alias).
- `database_name` (String) Database/schema name to connect to.
- `database_type` (String) Database type/driver, e.g. mysql, postgres, oracle, sqlserver. Maps to ServiceNow's jdbc_driver column.
- `mid_selection` (String) Decides which MID server is used. auto_select or specific_mid_server.
- `mid_server` (String) Sys ID of associated MID server if a specific MID server is selected.
- `use_mid_server` (Boolean) If true, the JDBC connection will be routed through a MID server.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the sys_id of the record in ServiceNow.
terraform import servicenow_jdbc_connection.example <sys_id>
```
